name: skill-creator-ms
description: Guide for creating effective skills for AI coding agents working with Azure SDKs and Microsoft Foundry services. Use when creating new skills or updating existing skills.
capabilities: Guide users through creating skills for Azure SDKs in Python, .NET, Java, and TypeScript
Provide structured templates for skill organization with frontmatter, body, and bundled resources
Document product area categories for skill classification
Create acceptance criteria and test scenarios for skill validation
Show symlink-based categorization for skill discoverability
author: sickn33
securitySummary: Static scanner flagged 219 potential issues (external commands, network, filesystem, env access) but ALL are false positives. The skill is a documentation/guide containing markdown code examples for creating Azure SDK skills. No executable malicious code exists. The skill explicitly warns against hardcoding credentials as a security anti-pattern.
valueStatement: This skill guides AI agents through creating modular knowledge packages for Azure SDKs and Microsoft Foundry services, ensuring consistent patterns and proper authentication.
userTitle: Create Azure SDK Skills for AI Agents
limitations: Requires users to provide SDK package name and documentation URL
Focused specifically on Azure SDKs and Microsoft Foundry, not general-purpose skills
Does not execute code - provides guidance only
Skill creation workflow assumes familiarity with Azure services

useCases.targetUser: Developers working with Azure services who want to create reusable skills
useCases.title: New Azure SDK Skill Creation
useCases.description: When a developer needs to create a skill for a new Azure service SDK

useCases.targetUser: Maintainers of Azure SDK skill libraries
useCases.title: Existing Skill Update
useCases.description: Updating existing skills with current Azure SDK patterns and authentication

useCases.targetUser: QA engineers ensuring skill reliability and correctness
useCases.title: Skill Testing and Validation
useCases.description: Creating acceptance criteria and test scenarios to validate skill quality

promptTemplates.title: Create Azure AI Agents Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the skill-creator-ms to create a new skill for the Azure AI Agents SDK. The SDK package is azure-ai-agents and the documentation is at https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-services/agents/.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a new skill for Azure AI Agents service

promptTemplates.title: Create Cosmos DB Skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a skill for Azure Cosmos DB using the azure-cosmos SDK. Documentation: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cosmos-db/
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating a skill for Azure Cosmos DB

promptTemplates.title: Add Authentication Section
promptTemplates.prompt: Add proper authentication documentation to the existing azure-storage skill using DefaultAzureCredential.
promptTemplates.scenario: Updating existing skill with proper auth patterns

promptTemplates.title: Create Test Scenarios
promptTemplates.prompt: Create acceptance criteria and test scenarios for the azure-eventhub skill following the skill-creator-ms patterns.
promptTemplates.scenario: Creating tests for an Azure SDK skill

bestPractices: Always use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication instead of hardcoding API keys
Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines and use progressive disclosure with reference files
Include both correct and incorrect code patterns in acceptance criteria to help validate outputs
antiPatterns: Creating a skill without first obtaining the SDK package name and documentation URL from the user
Hardcoding credentials in example code - always use environment variables
Skipping the authentication section - agents will improvise poorly without guidance

faq.question: What information do I need to provide before creating an Azure SDK skill?
faq.answer: You must provide the SDK package name (e.g., azure-ai-agents), the Microsoft Learn documentation URL, and optionally the GitHub repository for code patterns.

faq.question: How do I organize skills by category?
faq.answer: Create a symlink in skills/<language>/<category>/ pointing to .github/skills/<skill-name>. Categories include foundry, data, messaging, monitoring, identity, security, integration, compute, and container.

faq.question: What authentication method should I use?
faq.answer: Always use DefaultAzureCredential for Azure SDKs. Never hardcode credentials. Use environment variables like AZURE_ENDPOINT.

faq.question: How long should SKILL.md be?
faq.answer: Keep SKILL.md under 500 lines. Use progressive disclosure - load metadata always, body when skill triggers, and reference files as needed.

faq.question: Do I need to create tests for the skill?
faq.answer: Yes, every skill must have acceptance criteria in references/acceptance-criteria.md and test scenarios in tests/scenarios/<skill-name>/scenarios.yaml.

faq.question: What programming languages are supported?
faq.answer: The skill covers Python, .NET (C#), Java, and TypeScript patterns for Azure SDKs.
